title: The Big Blackout in Spain and Portugal: What Happened and Why?
summary: In 2025, Spain and Portugal had a big blackout where all the lights went out. A report found that the electrical grid had problems with vibrations called oscillations. These vibrations made some power equipment disconnect too early. The grid operators worked hard to fix it quickly. The report says that rules and policies in Spain made the grid weak and at risk. It suggests changes to prevent future blackouts. Data from power stations and solar panels helped experts understand what went wrong. They learned that managing different types of power, like reactive power, is important to keep the grid stable. This event shows why we need safe and strong electricity systems.

keywords.explanation: when all the lights and power go out in an area
vibrations or swings in the electrical grid that can cause problems
the network of wires and stations that brings electricity to homes
when equipment stops working and turns off from the grid
a type of electricity that doesn't do useful work but helps the grid run
rules and plans made by governments or companies

background_read: Electricity comes from power plants through a grid of wires to our homes. Sometimes, problems like vibrations or bad rules can cause blackouts, where everything goes dark. Experts study these events to make the grid better and safer for everyone.
Article_Structure: WHO: Grid operators and experts from ENTSO-e
WHAT: A big blackout in Spain and Portugal caused by oscillations and early disconnections
WHERE: The Iberian Peninsula (Spain and Portugal)
WHY: Policies and rules made the grid weak and at risk, leading to the blackout

perspectives.description: The blackout shows we need stronger rules to keep electricity safe.
